entitlement_changes: Claude Fable 5 access is listed for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plan users.
Max, Team, and Enterprise access is included through 2026-06-22 and requires usage credits starting 2026-06-23.
Pro users have limited daily access.
detail.new_terms: Fable 5 access varies by Anthropic subscription plan; Max, Team, and Enterprise users need usage credits after the included-access window, and Pro users receive limited daily access.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official announcement reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: usage credits required after the included-access window ends on 2026-06-22

impacts.recommended_action: Budget Fable 5 usage credits before June 23, 2026 and avoid assuming Max plan access remains included after the free-access window.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: usage credits required after the included-access window ends on 2026-06-22

impacts.recommended_action: Update team rollout plans and budget approvals before broad Fable 5 adoption after June 22, 2026.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: usage credits required after the included-access window ends on 2026-06-22

impacts.recommended_action: Confirm enterprise usage-credit terms with the account owner before making Fable 5 the default for internal or customer-facing workflows.
Treat Pro-plan Fable 5 access as limited daily capacity and keep fallback model routes available for repeated or long-running tasks.

limitations: APW records subscription-access facts from the official announcement, not account-specific contract terms.
This event does not assert API, cloud-provider, or regional access terms for Fable 5.
summary: Anthropic's June 9 announcement says Claude Fable 5 is available to Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plan users, with Max, Team, and Enterprise access included through June 22, 2026 and usage credits required starting June 23, 2026.
title: Anthropic Set Fable 5 Free Access Window And Usage Credit Requirement

detail.migration_notes: This event records approval-gated Mythos 5 availability. APW does not assert broad public availability, account eligibility, region coverage, rate limits, or workload suitability.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official announcement reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

impacts.recommended_action: Verify Project Glasswing or trusted-partner approval, model id availability, quota, and routing policy before adding Claude Mythos 5 to production allowlists.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: official announcement lists Claude Mythos 5 at USD 15 per million input tokens and USD 75 per million output tokens

impacts.recommended_action: Benchmark cost and token usage separately from Claude Fable 5 before exposing Mythos 5 to agent or long-context workloads.

limitations: APW records normalized factual metadata only; no provider announcement body is copied.
Access is approval-gated and may not be available to ordinary Anthropic API accounts.
summary: Anthropic's June 9 announcement introduced Claude Mythos 5 with a 1M-token context window, 128k maximum output tokens, adaptive thinking, and approval-gated API availability for Project Glasswing participants and trusted partners.
title: Anthropic Introduced Claude Mythos 5 For Trusted Access

examples: Run token counting with model claude-fable-5 before changing cost budgets.
Test fallback chains because a refused request can rerun on another model with different billing.
detail.new_accounting: Claude Fable 5 uses the Opus 4.7 tokenizer family, no-output refusals are not billed, fallback retries are billed at fallback-model rates, and the model is not available under zero data retention.
detail.old_accounting: Pre-Fable Anthropic routes may have used older tokenizer assumptions, no Fable-specific refusal category, and existing retention eligibility rules.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official release notes reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.basis: official release-note statements about tokenizer and fallback billing
estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: token counts can increase versus models before Claude Opus 4.7; fallback requests are billed at the fallback model rates

impacts.recommended_action: Re-run token counting and cost forecasts before routing Fable 5 traffic, especially for long-context workloads and fallback chains.
Keep zero-retention workloads off Fable 5 until the provider documents support for those accounts or an approved exception exists.

limitations: APW records provider-stated accounting semantics, not measured invoice deltas.
No raw release-note body is copied into the event.
summary: Anthropic's June 9 Claude Platform release notes say Claude Fable 5 uses the Opus 4.7 tokenizer family, adds refusal and fallback handling semantics, and requires 30-day data retention on the Claude API.
title: Anthropic Listed Fable 5 Tokenizer, Fallback Billing, And Retention Constraints

title: Anthropic Launched Claude Fable 5 On The Claude API

detail.migration_notes: Anthropic release notes recommend moving away from the Claude Opus 4.1 API model before the scheduled retirement date. Validate quality, cost, and parameter compatibility on replacement models before changing defaults.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official release notes reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

impacts.recommended_action: Remove claude-opus-4-1-20250805 from production routing, eval baselines, fallback chains, and customer-visible model selectors before August 5, 2026.

limitations: This event records the Claude API retirement signal only; cloud-provider hosted availability can have separate lifecycle notices.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official status page reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

examples: Use tools[].max_tokens to cap tool output on short-review workflows.
Classify no-output refusal responses separately from billed completion responses.
detail.new_accounting: Tool definitions can cap output with max_tokens, and Claude API requests that return stop_reason=refusal before output generation are not billed.
detail.old_accounting: Tool calls could produce full-length outputs unless constrained elsewhere; refusal billing needed local invoice verification.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official release notes reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.basis: official release-note statements about tool max_tokens and no-output refusal billing
estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: bounded tool output and no billing for refusals before output generation

impacts.recommended_action: Set tool max_tokens for workloads that do not need full-length tool responses and update billing attribution for no-output refusals.
Expose tool max_tokens in API wrappers and make refusal billing logic depend on whether output tokens were generated.

limitations: APW records provider-stated billing behavior and does not reconcile invoices.
No raw release-note text is committed.
summary: Anthropic's June 2 Claude Platform release notes list a tool-level max_tokens cap for output control and state that no-output refusal responses on the Claude API are no longer billed.
title: Anthropic Added Tool max_tokens And No-Output Refusal Billing Relief

detail.change_summary: Anthropic announced Claude Opus 4.8 along with Claude Code dynamic workflows, effort controls, higher long-running-agent capacity, and cheaper fast mode.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official announcement reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

impacts.recommended_action: Review Claude Code workflows that assume single-agent or short-running execution, and add usage controls before enabling larger dynamic workflow runs.
Check account and plan limits before treating larger Claude Code runs as free additional capacity.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: fast mode for Opus 4.8 is advertised as three times cheaper than previous models

impacts.recommended_action: Separate fast-mode usage from higher-effort usage when forecasting Claude Code spend and token-limit pressure.

limitations: APW records official announcement facts only; exact account-level rate limits and plan entitlements may vary.
summary: Anthropic announced Claude Opus 4.8 with Claude Code dynamic workflows, effort controls, expanded long-running agent behavior, and cheaper fast mode for affected users.
title: Anthropic Added Opus 4.8 Dynamic Workflows And Effort Controls
